display.purpose: Bricolage Grotesque. Page titles, wordmark, reveal headline.

title.purpose: Bricolage Grotesque. Section heads, hero names, the green CTA.

label.purpose: Inter, often uppercase. Tier names, counters, field labels.

shadows.name: debossed-pocket
shadows.purpose: Empty album slots; pressed into the page.

shadows.name: lifted-control
shadows.purpose: Primary buttons, filled stickers, nav arrows.

shadows.name: book
shadows.purpose: The album cover and the connect-screen pack.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: All transitions and entrances; exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Color and transform state changes on controls.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Pack reveal card flips and the album page turn.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one loud action on a screen: rip a pack, connect, start album.

components.name: Counter Button
components.description: Quiet secondary action (claim, buy) that sits beside the hero without competing.

components.name: Coin Pill
components.description: The fungible balance, shown as a quantity in a brand tint.

components.name: Sticker (common)
components.description: The collectible: a faced 3:4 card with name, rarity tier, and glyph.

components.name: Sticker (legendary, foil)
components.description: The legendary rarity treatment: foil-gold face under a moving holographic sheen.

components.name: Empty Album Pocket
components.description: An unfilled album slot, debossed into the leaf and waiting.

narrative.overview: The Panini album you filled at recess, rebuilt for the browser: warm paper, a forest-green cover, foil on the rare ones, and the thrill of pressing a sticker into its slot. Light theme because the scene is a bright desk, not a trading terminal. Everything is tactile. The audience is developers, so the craft is real, but the feeling is play; the interface teaches the fungibility spectrum by contrast, not by label. A coin reads as a quantity, a sticker as an object with a face and rarity, the album as the one unique thing you own.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper light theme, every neutral tinted toward the green brand hue.
One committed accent (forest leaf-green) plus a deliberate three-tier rarity palette.
Chunky grotesque display voice over a quiet, dependable sans for functional UI.
Tactile depth: things press in, lift, and stamp; motion is bold at the moments that matter.

rules.name: The Three Kinds Rule
rules.body: A coin, a sticker, and the album must never read as the same kind of element. The visual gap between them is the lesson.

rules.name: The Rarity-Is-Data Rule
rules.body: Rare blue and foil gold belong to stickers only. Never spent on buttons, chrome, or decoration.

rules.name: The Quiet-Controls Rule
rules.body: Display type is for wordmark, titles, and celebrations. Buttons, inputs, data, and labels stay Inter.

rules.name: The Pressed-Paper Rule
rules.body: Empty equals pressed in (inset shadow). Earned or actionable equals lifted out (drop shadow). Flat with neither is unfinished.

dos: Do keep the coin a quantity, the sticker an object, the album the one unique thing.
Do tint every neutral toward the green brand hue; never pure black or white.
Do reserve Rare Blue and Foil Gold for stickers only.
Do press empty things in and lift earned things out.
Do spend the bold motion budget on the pack rip, the stick, a legendary pull, a completed page; respect prefers-reduced-motion.
Do keep functional UI in Inter; let Bricolage carry personality in titles and celebrations.
donts: Don't drift toward generic web3: no neon on black, no glow, no glassmorphism.
Don't build a corporate SaaS dashboard: no identical card grids, no hero-metric tiles, no purple gradients.
Don't tip into childish: no Comic Sans energy, no primary-color overload, no balloon buttons.
Don't use a colored side-stripe border as an accent.
Don't put a gradient on text or a display font on a functional label.
Don't animate layout properties or use bounce/elastic easing.
